pathways: CLE-CLV meristem-size feedback (ZmCLE7->FEA2/CT2; ZmFCP1->FEA3; FEA4 bZIP; ZmCRN partner; TD1 CLV1-like)
Ramosa inflorescence branching (RA1 C2H2; RA2 LOB; RA3 TPP/T6P; REL2 TOPLESS)
AP2-like / spikelet-pair regulators (IDS1 = KRN1 = Ts6; SID1)
TB1 / GT1 / UB SBP-TF branching (ZmTB1; GT1; UB2; UB3 = KRN4; TSH4; TRU1)
Auxin biosynthesis + transport (VT2 TAA-like; SPI1 YUC-like; BIF1; BIF2 PINOID-like; BA1; BA2; ZmPIN1a/b)
Cytokinin (ABPH1 type-A ARR; ZmIPT biosynthesis; ZmCKX degradation; LOG1; Type-A/B ARR)
ERECTA-EPFL signaling (ZmER1/2/L; ZmEPFL peptide ligands) - 2026 addition
Cloned KRN QTL genes (KRN1 = IDS1/Ts6; KRN2 = WD40; KRN4 = UB3 distal CRE; KNR6; KRN5b)
Sugar / T6P signalling to branching (RA3 TPP activity)
canonical_mutants: fea2, fea3, fea4, ct2, Zmcrn, td1, fea2;fea3, fea2;fea4 double; fea2/ct2/Zmcrn heterozygotes
Bif3 (ZmWUS1 tandem duplication), kn1, Kn1-N GOF; ZmWUS1/2 CRISPR
ra1, ra2, ra3, rel2; rel2;ra1, rel2;ra2, rel2;ra1;ra2
ids1 = krn1, sid1, ids1;sid1, Ts6 GOF, ts4
ub2, ub3, ub2;ub3, ub2;ub3;tsh4, UB3 OE
vt2, spi1, spi1;vt2, bif1, bif2, bif1;bif2, ba1, ba2, ZmPIN1 RNAi
abph1, Abphyl2 dominant, ZmCKX OE, ZmIPT OE
KRN2 CRISPR, KRN4 absence allele, KNR6 OE/RNAi, AGAP CRISPR, KRN5b KO
Treatments: exogenous ZmCLE7/ZmFCP1 peptide, IAA, NAA, TIBA, NPA, kinetin, BA (cytokinin)
crosstalk: FEA2/FEA3 ZmCLE pathway feedback onto ZmWUS1 organising center
RA2 -> RA1 linear cascade with RA3 T6P lateral input
UB2/UB3 regulate IDS1 and cytokinin biosynthesis
Auxin <-> Cytokinin antagonism in IM (ABPH1 feedback)
KN1 <-> auxin <-> GA: KN1 represses GA biosynthesis and promotes auxin/CK biosynthesis

gaps.description: Du et al 2020 PLoS Genetics (10.1371/journal.pgen.1008764) was marked status:read but yielded 0 edges in Step 1. This paper is the canonical reference establishing the KRN4 distal enhancer -> UB3 chromatin-looping mechanism via UB2-centered complexes. Its omission is a large gap — the UB2->UB3 direct binding edge was not curated.
gaps.biological_justification: Du 2020 provides ChIP-seq + EMSA evidence that UB2 directly binds both the KRN4 region (a distal enhancer) and the UB3 promoter. This is the mechanism behind the well-known KRN4 QTL (3-fold UB3 expression difference between KRN4 alleles; ~2 rows KRN difference in H21 background).
search_queries: Du 2020 PLoS Genetics KRN4 UB3 UB2 ChIP-seq

gaps.description: KRN4 and UB3 are the SAME GENE (natural variation at UB3's distal cis-regulatory element, also known as KRN4, was cloned as the KRN4 QTL by Liu et al 2015). In the Step 1 repository, KRN4 appears only in perturbation T042 (KRN4_absence_allele) and is not annotated as an alias of UB3 at the node level. No duplicate node was created (KRN4 is NOT in any curated edge), but Builder-level node_annotations should record KRN4 as a synonym for UB3 so downstream reconciliation does not treat them as separate loci.
gaps.biological_justification: Liu 2015 PLoS Gen and Du 2020 PLoS Gen both establish that KRN4 is the UB3 distal enhancer, not an independent gene. Without the alias, the PERTURBATION agent might re-invent a KRN4 node.
gaps.resolution: Alias flagged for BUILDER to add to node_annotations.json; no new edge/node needed because curated_edges already uses UB3 consistently and T042 references KRN4 with correct biology

gaps.description: BA2 (barren stalk2, bHLH heterodimer partner of BA1) is entirely missing as a node and has no ba2 perturbation test. BA1 is extracted but BA1's obligate partner and the BA1-BA2 heterodimer physical interaction were missing.
gaps.biological_justification: Yao et al 2019 Molecular Plant established BA2 as a bHLH co-ortholog of LAX2 that heterodimerizes with BA1 in the nucleus and is required for reproductive axillary meristem development. ba2 mutants fail to produce ears, matching the canonical mutant checklist.
search_queries: maize BA1 BA2 bHLH heterodimer barren stalk axillary meristem

gaps.description: SPI1 was source-only — its targets were extracted but its upstream regulators (KN1 direct binding of YUC auxin biosynthesis genes) were not.
gaps.biological_justification: Bolduc et al 2012 Genes & Development ChIP-seq + RNA-seq defined KN1 as a direct regulator of auxin biosynthesis, with KN1 binding YUC-family and TAA-family loci, as well as PIN efflux transporters and AFB auxin coreceptors. Step 1 extracted KN1->VT2 but missed the parallel KN1->SPI1, KN1->ZmPIN1a, KN1->TIR1/AFB, and KN1->ZmIPT edges from the same paper.
search_queries: KN1 ChIP-seq direct targets auxin biosynthesis SPI1 VT2 maize Bolduc Hake
gaps.resolution: SPI1 no longer source-only (in=1 from KN1); also added KN1->ZmPIN1a and KN1->TIR1 from the same paper

gaps.description: ZmFCP1 was source-only. Candidate upstream regulators explored: FEA4 ChIP-seq (Pautler 2015) and developmental repressors.
gaps.biological_justification: Checked Pautler 2015 Plant Cell FEA4 ChIP-seq / RNA-seq. Authors explicitly state 'FEA4 does not appear to directly target any of the core CLV-WUS machinery' and no ChIP peaks on ZmCLE7/ZmFCP1 loci are reported. ZmFCP1 transcription appears to be spatially regulated in the organizing center (co-expressed with FEA4 and ZmLOG7) without a clearly curated TF regulator in Zea literature.
search_queries: FEA4 ChIP-seq direct targets maize meristem ZmCLE7 ZmFCP1 Pautler
gaps.unclosable_reason: No maize paper was found in the second round that assigns a direct transcriptional regulator to ZmFCP1. In Arabidopsis, CLE14/CLE20 (FCP1 orthologs) are regulated developmentally rather than by a single TF. Leave as biological source-node for BUILDER.
gaps.residual_for_builder: Flag ZmFCP1 as a biological source node (is_source candidate)

gaps.description: TB1 is the founding domestication gene but had only 4 edges. Direct TB1 targets TRU1 and GT1 were already captured; also cross-checked for any additional targets.
gaps.biological_justification: Dong 2017 PNAS established TB1 directly binds the tru1 first intron and last exon (ChIP + EMSA) and positively regulates tru1. TB1 also upregulates gt1 (Whipple 2011). Both edges were already present (TB1->TRU1 +, TB1->GT1 +). No new edges added but re-read confirmed sign and mechanism strength.
search_queries: TB1 teosinte branched1 direct targets TRU1 GT1 maize
gaps.resolution: Already captured; no new edges needed. TB1 coverage sufficient for domestication axis.

gaps.description: UB3 had 6 edges but missed the UB2 upstream binding (now G001) and the UB3 -> cytokinin axis (now G008). After G001+G008 closure UB3 now has in=2, out=6.
gaps.biological_justification: UB3 is the canonical KRN QTL gene. Du 2020 establishes UB2->UB3 direct binding via KRN4 enhancer (closed G001). Du 2017 establishes UB3 -> CK biosynthesis/signaling repression (closed G008).
search_queries: UB3 direct binding upstream regulators maize

gaps.description: UB3 -> Cytokinin repression loop (UB3 directly binds LOG1 and Type-A ARR promoters) was not captured. This is the mechanism linking SBP branching TFs to CK homeostasis.
gaps.biological_justification: Du et al 2017 New Phytologist 214:721-733 shows UB3 binds promoters of LOG1 (CK-activating enzyme) and Type-A ARR (CK signaling attenuator). In ub3 mutants, CK biosynthesis genes are up and degradation genes are down, producing the increased-branching / altered-KRN phenotype.
search_queries: UB3 cytokinin biosynthesis signaling maize rice

gaps.description: Cytokinin biosynthesis node ZmIPT was underrepresented (1 edge) with no upstream regulator. Bolduc 2012 shows KN1 directly binds and positively regulates the rice LOG ortholog (CK-activating enzyme in the IPT/LOG pathway).
gaps.biological_justification: KN1 maintains indeterminate meristem identity partly by up-regulating CK biosynthesis. In maize, KN1 binding of the LOG ortholog is evidence for the KN1->ZmIPT regulatory edge.
search_queries: KN1 LOG cytokinin biosynthesis ChIP-seq maize

gaps.description: ZmCKX (CK degradation) had only 2 edges and no OE-in-ear experimental test. No new maize paper was surfaced in the second round that specifically reported ear-targeted ZmCKX overexpression with KRN quantification.
gaps.biological_justification: Bartrina et al 2011 Arabidopsis work is canonical but not maize. Maize ZmCKX OE exists in leaf-senescence context (Gong 2022 Frontiers) but not clearly in ear meristem with KRN outcome.
search_queries: ZmCKX overexpression maize kernel row number ear meristem
gaps.unclosable_reason: No maize-specific ear-targeted ZmCKX OE paper with KRN quantification found in OA sources. BUILDER should treat ZmCKX as a low-confidence degradation node.
gaps.residual_for_builder: ZmCKX coverage remains thin; test cases limited

gaps.description: The ZmER1/2/L receptor-kinase pathway and its ZmEPFL peptide ligands are an entirely missing pathway in Step 1. This pathway was published 2026-01-12 in Nature Communications and restricts IM size via ZmWUS1.
gaps.biological_justification: Liu et al 2026 Nature Communications (DOI 10.1038/s41467-025-67634-3) established three redundant ZmER receptor kinases that are activated by five redundant ZmEPFL peptide ligands. Loss-of-function produces enlarged IM and increased KRN, with ZmWUS1 identified as the downstream mediator. This adds a parallel arm to the CLE-CLV meristem-size feedback module.
search_queries: maize ERECTA ZmER inflorescence meristem ZmEPFL kernel row number CRISPR

gaps.description: The Wang 2019 New Phytologist paper cloned the krn1 QTL and mapped it to ids1/Ts6. KRN1 is NOT an independent gene — it IS IDS1. Step 1 has the IDS1 node with extensive edges and the Ts6 GOF test (T039) but does not annotate KRN1 as an IDS1 alias, which could mislead downstream agents.
gaps.biological_justification: Wang et al 2019 nph.15890 map-based cloning of krn1 major QTL = ids1 AP2 domain gene. Ts6 is a dominant GOF IDS1 allele. Same gene across all three names.
search_queries: KRN1 maize LBD domain kernel row number Wang 2019 New Phytologist
gaps.resolution: Alias flagged for BUILDER. No duplicate node exists because KRN1 never appears as a separate node.

gaps.description: Several canonical doubles/combinations missing: bif1;bif2 double, ba1;ba2 double, ZmPIN1a;ZmPIN1b RNAi double. These are named in primary literature but not as distinct tests in Step 1.
gaps.biological_justification: Galli et al 2015 already reports Bif1;Bif4 double (T055) and bif2 single (T056) — bif1;bif2 might be equivalent or not separately reported. Second-round search did not surface a cleanly quantified bif1;bif2 double KRN phenotype.
gaps.unclosable_reason: Bif1;Bif4 is documented (T055); bif1;bif2 independently is not clearly separable in Galli 2015. Leave as residual.
gaps.residual_for_builder: bif1;bif2 not extracted; BUILDER need not create this test

gaps.description: KRN2 (WD40 protein, convergent maize-rice selection) had 2 edges. Chen 2022 Science shows KRN2 interacts with DUF1644 to negatively regulate grain number but does not provide direct binding to UB3 or other KRN QTL genes.
gaps.biological_justification: KRN2-DUF1644 interaction is already captured in the Step 1 extraction. The mechanism by which this complex converges on UB3 / IM size is not resolved at promoter-binding level in Chen 2022. Leave as is.
search_queries: KRN2 WD40 maize UB3 KRN4 Chen 2022 Science mechanism
gaps.resolution: KRN2-DUF1644 complex already captured; no further edges supported by current literature

gaps.description: KNR6 (out=3, in=0) and miR156 (out=3, in=0) remain source-only after merge.
gaps.biological_justification: KNR6 is a serine/threonine kinase; its direct upstream transcriptional regulation is not curated in Jia 2020 Nat Comm. miR156 is developmentally regulated by sugar/phase-change signals but maize-specific regulators are not comprehensively published.
gaps.unclosable_reason: No well-characterized maize transcriptional regulator for KNR6 or for miR156 was surfaced.
gaps.residual_for_builder: Flag both as biological source-node candidates for BUILDER (is_source likely true)

bidirectional_audit.resolution: 1 of 4 source-only nodes resolved (SPI1 gained in-edge from KN1). ZmFCP1 appears to be a biological source node (no curated direct TF regulator in maize literature). KNR6 and miR156 also appear to be biological source nodes — flag for BUILDER is_source:true

step_1_papers_with_zero_edges_but_status: 10.1186/gb-2008-9-1-204 (Banks 2008 miR172 review - used in T040 but no edges)
extraction_density_audit.comment: The Du 2020 PLoS Gen paper flagged status:read in Step 1 yielded 0 edges despite being the canonical KRN4-UB3 enhancer reference. Re-read recovered 1 critical direct-binding edge. Bolduc 2012 (KN1 ChIP-seq) had 5 edges but a re-read recovered 4 additional edges for auxin biosynthesis / transport / receptor / CK biosynthesis that were in the Results but not extracted. The remaining 0-edge papers are reviews or transcriptome papers that contribute evidence to existing edges rather than mechanistic new edges.

duplicate_node_consolidations.comment: No duplicate-node bug was present. KRN4 does not appear as a separate node in curated_edges.json — it only shows up in perturbation test T042 (KRN4_absence_allele) with correct biology. Similarly, KRN1 does not exist as a separate node — T039 (Ts6) correctly uses the IDS1 gene. No merge was needed; only BUILDER-level node_annotations should record the aliases so downstream reconciliation is unambiguous.

residual_gaps_for_builder: ZmFCP1 has no curated transcriptional regulator in maize literature. Treat as biological source node (is_source:true). Flag as literature_gap.
KNR6 has no curated transcriptional regulator. Treat as biological source node.
miR156 has no curated direct transcriptional regulator in maize. Treat as biological source node.
ZmCKX ear-targeted OE experiment with KRN quantification is not in maize literature. Degradation-enzyme coverage is thin.
Alias annotation: UB3 has alias KRN4 (add to node_annotations.json for downstream clarity).
Alias annotation: IDS1 has aliases KRN1 and Ts6 (add to node_annotations.json).
ZmWUS2 paralog has no curated edges (only Bif3 = ZmWUS1 tandem duplication is in network). May be absent due to lack of published direct-binding data.
